steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures on each side of the blank
steps.content:
Before the blank: independent clause (“entered San Francisco”).
After the blank: participial phrase (“having left...”) followed by an independent clause (“the celebrated ship set a record...”).

steps.title: Apply the comma-splice rule
steps.content:
Two independent clauses cannot be connected by just a comma.

The correct answer uses a period to separate them and structures the second sentence as a participial phrase (Having left...) followed by a comma and the main clause—the standard punctuation for a participial phrase at sentence start.

The remaining choices either create comma splices or ungrammatical constructions.

That confirms B is correct.
